evidence_quotes.quote: I wholeheartedly believe it would be beneficial if the Digital Fairness Act prevented video game publishers from rendering video games inoperable.

While it is not reasonable to expect companies that sell digital products to offer support to customers in perpetuity, in my opinion companies should not be allowed to remove users' access to said products and render them inoperable as they see fit, especially once the product has been fully paid for.

paragraphs.text: I wholeheartedly believe it would be beneficial if the Digital Fairness Act prevented video game publishers from rendering video games inoperable. While it is not reasonable to expect companies that sell digital products to offer support to customers in perpetuity, in my opinion companies should not be allowed to remove users' access to said products and render them inoperable as they see fit, especially once the product has been fully paid for. Once support for a video game ends, the publisher should leave it in a functional state and let the customers decide if they want to tackle any future problems arising from software or hardware-related incompatibilities.
